topic: Acidic food and high-acid wine balance
trigger_keywords: vino blanco joven con alta acidez

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A dish containing substantial acidity, such as vinegar, citrus juice or ceviche, creates a strongly acidic reference point during tasting.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Against that reference, a wine with insufficient acidity can lose definition and seem broad or flat, whereas a high-acid wine retains freshness and structural balance.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: A young high-acid white wine is therefore a reliable match for a strongly acidic dish because neither component makes the other seem dull or unbalanced.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: match an acidic dish with a wine of sufficient acidity so the wine continues to taste fresh and balanced beside the food.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
